name: Scallops in Adobo
recipeIngredient: 2 cups fresh or frozen corn kernels
3 poblano peppers, roasted and peeled, cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the ancho chiles in hot water for 1 hour. Drain and save the water.
2. Seed the chiles and blend them with the rest of the adobo ingredients (garlic, cumin, oregano, marjoram, cinnamon, vinegar, orange juice, and salt) until smooth. Add chile water as needed.
3. Transfer the blended mixture to a bowl. Toss the scallops in the adobo sauce and refrigerate for 4 hours.
4. Lift the scallops out of the adobo and let the excess fall off. Heat olive oil over high heat in a skillet and saute the scallops until seared, about 1 minute per side.
5. Add corn, poblano peppers, green onions, and tomatoes to the skillet. Stir until the scallops are cooked, approximately 4 minutes.
6. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve over green rice.
Note: Adobo is a general name for chile or chile-tomato sauces heavily spiced with vinegar and/or citrus juices and spices. If serving with wine, be cautious with acidic ingredients; otherwise, serve with beer.
